flashcards.definition: A persistent body of ice formed from compacted snow that moves under its own weight; stores ~69% of Earth's fresh water.
Continental-scale glacier covering >50,000 km²; only two exist today: Greenland and Antarctica.
Accumulation of glacial sediment (till) deposited at the edges, bottom, or terminus of a glacier.
A valley carved by glacial erosion, wider and flatter-bottomed than the V-shaped valleys carved by rivers.
When a glacier's terminus melts back because melting exceeds accumulation; accelerating globally due to climate change.
